name: Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop
name_original: Montes de Toledo por la subida a los Baños de Robledillo por la Sierra de San Pablo — circular por San Pablo de los Montes
description: Tackle the difficult 39.4-mile Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop, a road cycling route with 3122 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera offers diverse terrain within the Montes de Toledo mountain range. The region features rolling hills and varied landscapes, providing a range of challenges for cyclists. Proximity to Cabañeros National Park indicates a rich natural environment, with historical elements like ancient granite quarries adding to the scenic routes. Best road cycling routes around Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera The most popular road cycling route is Montes de Toledo: Alto de las Paradas Loop, a 59.5 miles (95.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Montes de Toledo. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Pablo de los Montes and El Robledillo Pass Loop, a moderate 33.7 miles (54.2 km) path. This route navigates through varied terrain, including mountain passes. Local road cyclists also love the Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop, a 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail leading through the Sierra de San Pablo, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera is defined by rolling hills, mountain passes, and scenic natural areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera?
A: Komoot features 8 distinct road cycling routes around Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents through the Montes de Toledo.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: The road cycling routes around Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera are characterized by rolling hills and varied landscapes within the Montes de Toledo mountain range. You'll encounter mountain passes and scenic natural areas, providing a diverse challenge for cyclists.

Q: Are there options for less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. The region offers a network of paths with options for different ability levels, ensuring that less experienced cyclists can also enjoy the scenery.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the routes in Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera?
A: The routes in Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Over 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty and diverse challenges.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: Absolutely. The area includes 2 difficult road cycling routes with significant elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366253497/montes-de-toledo-alto-de-las-paradas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo: Alto de las Paradas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 59.5-mile route with over 1,100 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can experience the picturesque Montes de Toledo and the rich natural environment near Cabañeros National Park. Routes may pass by historical elements like ancient granite quarries. You might also encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledillo Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menasalbas Village Center&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers scenic vistas. A notable viewpoint is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5899115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Eagle&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023129028/san-pablo-de-los-montes-and-el-robledillo-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pablo de los Montes and El Robledillo Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366256914/sierra-de-san-pablo-and-robledillo-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming villages or historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of natural beauty and cultural exploration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029256206/circular-from-pulgar-via-menasalbas-las-vent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular from Pulgar via Menasalbas, Las Vent&lt;/a&gt; route passes through Menasalbas, a village with its own distinct character. The region's history, including ancient granite quarries, also adds a unique dimension to the scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in this region?
A: The region's fields are particularly vibrant with color in May, making it a beautiful time for cycling. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: What are the typical distances for road cycling routes in the area?
A: Road cycling routes around Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera vary in length. You can find routes ranging from around 30 miles (50 km) to nearly 60 miles (95 km). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menasalbas Village Center – Menasalbas loop from Cuerva&lt;/a&gt; is about 42.8 km (26.6 miles).

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of these routes?
A: While specific parking information for each route is not detailed, Las Ventas Con Peña Aguilera is a village that caters to outdoor activities. It's generally possible to find parking in or near the village centers, which often serve as starting points for routes.
tips: Menasalbas is a charming village in the Montes de Toledo mountains, ideal for those seeking history, nature, and tranquility. It stands out for its architectural heritage, hiking trails, and proximity to the Torcón Reservoir and Cabañeros National Park.

This town at the foot of the Sierra de San Pablo, at the eastern end of the Montes de Toledo, celebrates the Fiesta de la Vaca every January 24 and 25, declared of Regional Tourist Interest. On the night of January 24, a bonfire is lit to warm the neighbors, who jump over it while they sing, dance and drink chocolate. The next day they eat tostones (roasted chickpeas) and drink lemonade, after having run in front of the "cow", which is a stick with horns that chases the participants.
Beautiful road to climb the Puerto del Robledillo, with good road surface and little traffic. You will enjoy both the effort of going up and going down without cars.

Las Ventas con Peña Aguilera is a charming village in the Montes de Toledo mountains, ideal for rural getaways, hiking, and cultural tourism. Travelers highlight its spectacular scenery, tranquility, game cuisine, and nationally renowned leather crafts. Furthermore, its proximity to Cabañeros National Park makes it a strategic location for enjoying both nature and tradition.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop by racebike?
faqs.answer: For a racebike, this loop typically takes around 3 hours and 6 minutes to complete. However, this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The road surfaces on this loop are generally well-paved, especially on the ascent to Robledillo Pass. While the Montes de Toledo region can sometimes feature unpaved segments, this specific route is designed for racebikes, offering a smooth ride for both climbing and descending.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' for racebiking, featuring significant elevation changes with approximately 951 meters of ascent. It requires a good to very good fitness level, making it more suitable for experienced cyclists rather than beginners.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several interesting spots. You'll pass through the Menasalbas Village Center, and the historic Fiesta de la Vaca in San Pablo de los Montes. A highlight for cyclists is the Robledillo Pass itself, known for its excellent road surface and beautiful views. You'll also ride past the Parish Church of San Pedro Apóstol in Las Ventas con Peña Aguilera.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, starting points for similar routes in the Montes de Toledo often include villages like San Pablo de los Montes or Menasalbas. Look for public parking options within these towns, which typically offer convenient access to the road network.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montes de Toledo region, where this loop is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons typically provide milder temperatures, which are ideal for tackling the climbs without extreme heat.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sierra de San Pablo and Robledillo Pass Loop?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, dogs are generally not permitted to accompany cyclists on the road for safety reasons. If you're looking for dog-friendly outdoor activities in the Montes de Toledo, consider exploring hiking trails where dogs on a leash might be allowed, but always check local regulations first.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the [CIMA TO03] El Robledillo (Baños de la Guarra) * San Pablo de los Montes, [CIMA TO03] El Robledillo (Baños de la Guarra) * Retuerta de Bullaque, and smaller segments of the Ruta de los Molinos and Camino de San Pablo de los Montes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Montes de Toledo region?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads within the Montes de Toledo region. However, always respect local regulations, especially if you venture into private land or protected natural areas.

faqs.question: What makes the Robledillo Pass a notable feature for cyclists?
faqs.answer: The Robledillo Pass is highly regarded by cyclists for its excellent road surface and relatively low traffic. This combination allows for a focused and enjoyable climbing experience, followed by a thrilling descent, without the common distractions of heavy vehicle traffic.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a racebike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and significant elevation, you should pack essentials like sufficient water and nutrition, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), appropriate cycling apparel for the weather, a helmet, and a fully charged phone. Consider bringing an extra layer for descents, even on warm days.
